data.title: White House to Host Third Stablecoin Yield Meeting
data.description: Eleanor Terrett posted on X. A third meeting focused on stablecoin yields is scheduled to occur at the White House tomorrow at 9:00 A.M. ET. The meeting will include a select group of representatives from the cryptocurrency and banking sectors. Further details are anticipated to be released in the morning.

data.title: Landslide at Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park Results in Fatality
data.description: A landslide occurred at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park on Wednesday, resulting in the death of one worker. Bloomberg posted on X, citing information from IMIP spokesperson Dedy Kurniawan. The incident highlights ongoing safety concerns in industrial areas. Further details about the cause of the landslide and measures being taken to prevent future occurrences have not been disclosed.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 610 USDT with a Narrowed 1.25% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2026, 04:32 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 610 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 610.119995 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.25% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: XRP Demand Defies Bear Market Predictions
data.description: Crypto KOL Eli5defi on X highlights the unexpected demand for XRP despite predictions of a bear market. Contrary to earlier claims that XRP lacked demand, recent developments suggest otherwise, challenging the notion of an impending bear market.

data.title: OpenAI Collaborates with Tata Group on AI Technologies in India
data.description: OpenAI has announced a partnership with the Tata Group to advance artificial intelligence technologies in India. Bloomberg posted on X that this collaboration includes the development of data center infrastructure, which has the potential to become one of the largest in the country. The partnership aims to leverage Tata Group's extensive experience in technology and infrastructure to enhance AI capabilities and support the growing demand for AI solutions in India. This move is expected to bolster India's position in the global AI landscape, fostering innovation and technological growth.

data.title: India's AI Ambitions Explored by Google DeepMind CEO
data.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis shared insights on India's potential to become a leader in artificial intelligence during a discussion with Bloomberg TV. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Hassabis's views on India's strategic approach to AI development. Hassabis emphasized the importance of innovation and collaboration in advancing AI technologies, noting India's growing influence in the global tech landscape. The conversation took place amid India's AI Summit, where industry leaders are gathering to discuss the future of AI and its impact on various sectors. Hassabis's remarks underscore the significance of India's role in shaping AI advancements and the opportunities for growth in this field.

data.title: Bill Gates Withdraws from Indian AI Summit Keynote
data.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates unexpectedly canceled his keynote address at a prominent AI summit in India just hours before he was scheduled to speak. Bloomberg posted on X that Gates' withdrawal from the event was announced without a detailed explanation for the sudden change in plans. The summit, which gathers leading figures in artificial intelligence, was set to feature Gates as a key speaker, highlighting the importance of AI in global technological advancements. The organizers have yet to comment on the impact of his absence on the event's proceedings.

data.title: Hong Kong to Issue Stablecoin Licenses in March to Boost Crypto Adoption
data.description:
Hong Kong legislator Ng Kit Chuang announced on February 19 that the city will issue its first batch of stablecoin licenses in March. According to BlockBeats, Ng expressed hope that the government would encourage the use of cryptocurrencies among Hong Kong residents through stablecoin airdrops.

Ng highlighted that three years ago, the government distributed electronic consumption vouchers worth HKD 10,000 to each citizen, significantly popularizing electronic payments. He suggested that once stablecoin issuers receive their licenses, the government could distribute stablecoin airdrops to eligible citizens for spending at local small and medium-sized enterprises, such as restaurants, entertainment venues, and cinemas. This initiative aims to enhance Hong Kong's status as a Web3 and digital asset hub, achieving both economic stimulation and increased cryptocurrency adoption.

Ng emphasized that this approach is not merely a government handout but a strategy to leverage stablecoin consumption vouchers to create a multiplier effect. The administrative costs would be covered by the licensed companies.

data.title: Japan's Proposed Food Tax Cut Raises Concerns Among Economists
data.description: A recent Reuters survey reveals that 57% of economists express varying levels of concern over Japan's proposed two-year food consumption tax reduction plan. According to Jin10, the initiative aims to alleviate financial pressure on households, but experts worry about its potential impact on government revenue and economic stability. The plan has sparked debate among policymakers and economists, highlighting the challenges of balancing fiscal policy with consumer relief efforts.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Economists Predict Japan's Intervention in Currency Market to Curb Yen Weakness
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Reuters reveals that 69% of economists anticipate Japan's intervention in the currency market to address the weakening yen. According to Jin10, the survey indicates that the USD/JPY exchange rate reaching 160 could likely trigger such intervention. The economists' consensus highlights concerns over the yen's depreciation and its potential impact on Japan's economy. The survey underscores the growing expectation for governmental action to stabilize the currency and mitigate adverse economic effects.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Expected to Raise Key Interest Rate by Mid-Year
data.description: A recent Reuters survey indicates that 58% of economists anticipate the Bank of Japan will increase its key interest rate to 1% by the end of June. According to Jin10, this marks a significant rise in expectations compared to a January survey, where only 36% of economists predicted such an adjustment. The potential rate hike reflects growing confidence in Japan's economic recovery and the central bank's efforts to manage inflation.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,069, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on these exchanges could reach $955 million. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $1,874, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions could amount to $404 million.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.76% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2026, 04:03 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,050.5625 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.76% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Polymarket Partners with Substack for Market Data Integration
data.description: Polymarket has announced an exclusive partnership with Substack. According to ChainCatcher, this collaboration will enable Substack authors to seamlessly integrate Polymarket's market data into their content. The initiative aims to embed predictive market data into the writing process, offering data-driven support to content creators.

data.title: US SEC Chairman: Key measures to be introduced in the coming months include investment contract recognition and exemptions for innovative tokenized securities trading.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) website published the discussion transcripts of Chairman Paul Atkins at the ETHDenver conference, revealing that the following issues will be considered in the coming weeks and months: 1. Developing a framework to explain how to view crypto assets bound by investment contracts, including how such contracts are entered into and terminated; 2. Establishing innovation exemptions to facilitate limited trading of certain tokenized securities on new platforms, with an eye toward establishing a long-term regulatory framework; 3. Introducing a rulemaking proposal aimed at establishing a reasonable pathway to raising funds through the sale of crypto assets; 4. Letters of no action and exemptions to provide more clarity, including addressing wallets that do not require registration under the Securities Exchange Act; 5. Rulemaking regarding broker-dealers holding non-securities crypto assets (including payments in stablecoins); 6. Developing rules on modernizing transfer agents to accommodate the role blockchain can play in record keeping; 7. Providing additional guidance and letters of no action to help the crypto community understand how existing rules apply to unique realities.

data.title: Ethereum Tokenization Soared 315% to $17B, Market Share Hit 34%
data.description:
Key Insights Capital allocation in the digital asset market is approaching a seismic change. Recent statistics show a significant increase in institutional activity related to blockchain-based assets. Ethereum has accelerated this transformation, with greater momentum and scale than ever before
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2026/02/18/ethereum-tokenization-soared-315-to-17b-market-share-hit-34/

data.title: Avalanche in Northern California Results in Multiple Fatalities and Missing Persons
data.description: An avalanche occurred in the mountainous region of Northern California on Tuesday morning, impacting a skiing group of 15 individuals. According to Ming Pao, local police reported on Wednesday that eight people have been confirmed dead, and one person remains missing as search efforts continue. Six individuals have been rescued.

data.title: Federal Reserve Backs Prediction Markets for Inflation Forecasting Amid Regulatory Challenges
data.description: Federal Reserve researchers have endorsed macro-focused prediction markets as effective tools for real-time forecasting of inflation and interest rates, suggesting they surpass traditional measures. According to NS3.AI, these markets offer a more dynamic approach to economic predictions. However, state regulators, particularly in Nevada, are intensifying enforcement actions against certain prediction markets due to concerns over gambling. Industry experts highlight that ongoing regulatory uncertainty may hinder the liquidity and stability required for these markets to reach their full potential as forecasting tools.

data.title: Polymarket and Substack have entered into an exclusive partnership to integrate market data into news content.
data.description: Polymarket has announced an exclusive partnership with Substack. According to the announcement, Substack authors will be able to natively integrate Polymarket market data into their content, providing real-time market forecasts to support news reporting. Both companies stated that this move aims to embed predictive market data into the writing process, providing content creators with data-driven support.

data.title: Clarity Act Approval Probability Rises to 90% on Polymarket
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that the probability of the Clarity Act's approval has surged to 90% on Polymarket. This development has sparked significant interest and discussion within the cryptocurrency community. The Clarity Act, which aims to provide clearer regulations for digital assets, has been a topic of debate among policymakers and industry stakeholders. The increased probability on Polymarket, a popular prediction market platform, suggests growing confidence in the Act's passage. Observers are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, given the potential implications for the cryptocurrency sector.
